channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Heavy Rains Increase Kenya's Hydropower Generation, Reducing Reliance on Thermal Power
item.description: Kenya has seen a boost in hydropower generation following sustained rains in the Mount Kenya and Aberdare regions. According to Kenya Electricity Generating Company (KenGen), output on May 4 reached 9...

item.title: Eldoret Family Seeks Help to Locate Son Living in Australia
item.description: A family from Eldoret, Kenya, is appealing for assistance to locate their son, Michael Kiplimo, who moved to Australia in 2014 seeking better opportunities. Michael, now 37, maintained regular communi...

item.title: Pakistan Army Cadet Recognised as Best Foreign Cadet at Australian Military College
item.description: Pakistan Army cadet Sardar Arsam Abbas, who is part of the 152nd Long Course at Pakistan Military Academy, has been declared the 'overall best foreign military cadet' by Australia’s Royal Military Col...

item.title: Kenya and Tanzania Deepen Energy Cooperation Through Major Power Interconnection Projects
item.description: President William Ruto of Kenya has commended the growing energy partnership between Kenya and Tanzania, saying joint projects are playing a big role in boosting economic growth and regional cooperati...

item.title: Tanzania and Kenya Sign Eight Agreements to Boost Regional Cooperation and Development
item.description: On 5th May 2026, Tanzanian President Dr Samia Suluhu Hassan and Kenyan President William Ruto witnessed the signing of eight key Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) aimed at enhancing bilateral relation...

item.title: US Claims Iran Conflict Has Ended Despite Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: In this episode of Decoding America, hosts Reged Ahmad and Jonathan Yerushalmy examine the White House's assertion that Operation Epic Fury has concluded, even though the blockade of the Strait of Hor...

item.title: BMW rolls out its 2-millionth electric vehicle from Dingolfing plant
item.description: BMW has reached a major milestone with the production of its 2-millionth battery electric vehicle (BEV) at the Dingolfing plant in Germany. The landmark vehicle is an i5 M60 xDrive sedan in Tansanit B...

item.title: Fans Criticise American Accents and Modern Language in Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' Trailer
item.description: Christopher Nolan's upcoming epic, 'The Odyssey', has sparked online debate following the release of its first trailer. The film, featuring Matt Damon as Odysseus, Tom Holland as Telemachus, and Anne ...

item.title: Google AI employees seek union recognition amid concerns over military applications of their technology
item.description: A group of Google AI employees is requesting formal recognition for a union, expressing concerns over how the company's artificial intelligence technology is being used by the Israeli and US military....

item.title: Knicks Win Game 1 Against 76ers with Strong Team Performance Led by Brunson
item.description: The New York Knicks opened their playoff series against the Philadelphia 76ers with a commanding 137-98 victory in Game 1. This win marked their fourth consecutive postseason game won by 16 points or ...

item.title: Alberta UCP Staff Observed Separatist Voter Database Prior to Its Shutdown
item.description: Alberta’s United Conservative Party (UCP) staff participated in a virtual meeting hosted by a separatist group, the Centurion Project, two weeks before the group’s voter database became the subject of...

item.title: ODM Has Not Yet Decided on Presidential Candidate for 2027, Says Otiende Amollo
item.description: Rarieda MP Otiende Amollo has clarified that the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) has not yet made any official decision regarding its strategy for the 2027 elections. Contrary to speculation, the par...

item.title: Pakistan Leaders Discuss Urgent Climate Actions and Financing at Breathe Pakistan Conference
item.description: The second Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference, organised by DawnMedia in Islamabad, brought together policymakers, experts, and stakeholders to address Pakistan’s climate vulnera...

item.title: Australia invests in fuel security amid rising shortages and imports reliance
item.description: The Australian government has announced plans to spend over A$10 billion to enhance the nation’s fuel security, including $3.7 billion to create publicly owned fuel reserves capable of holding one bil...

item.title: MILF-backed UBJP candidates submit COCs for inaugural BARMM parliamentary elections
item.description: COTABATO CITY – The United Bangsamoro Justice Party (UBJP), the political party of the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF), formally filed certificates of candidacy (COCs) for the upcoming first-ever...

item.title: Rapid Construction of Traveller Site in Willows Green Sparks Local Concern
item.description: Residents near Willows Green, Essex, have expressed alarm at the rapid development of a four-acre traveller site, describing the work as reminiscent of a 'military operation'. Construction began immed...

item.title: Tourist survives 150-feet fall at Sinhagad Fort during sunset photography
item.description: On Wednesday evening, a 35-year-old tourist named Yogesh Mohan Shitole from Shirur experienced a terrifying accident at the historic Sinhagad Fort in Pune, Maharashtra. While attempting to capture the...

item.title: Health Minister Motsoaledi Provides Update to Parliament on Hantavirus Situation
item.description: On Wednesday, Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi addressed Parliament and the media to provide a detailed update on the current Hantavirus situation in South Africa. During the briefing, Minister Motsoa...

item.title: Honda Suspends $15 Billion Electric Vehicle Plant Project in Canada
item.description: Honda has reportedly put its plans for a $15 billion electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing plant in Canada on hold. The decision comes amid a shifting automotive market and increasing uncertainty in the...

item.title: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif warns of stronger response to future India-Pakistan escalations
item.description: In a televised message marking what the government calls “Marka-i-Haq” (Battle of Truth), Defence Minister Khawaja Asif issued a strong warning regarding Pakistan’s future military posture. Referring ...
